_meta.description: Single source of truth for the Urban Planning in India artifacts. Edit values in 'datasets' (or via pipeline/refresh.js from registered sources), then run 'node pipeline/build.js' to thread the changes into every dashboard.

y1.meta: Low-capex moves deliverable within ~12 months — mostly operations, road-space reallocation and integration.

items.p: Prioritise routes that feed metro stations and underserved peripheries, pushing toward the upper end of the buses-per-lakh norm.

items.h: Bus-priority lanes on the 10 worst corridors
items.p: Paint-and-bollard priority lanes on ORR, Whitefield, Hosur Rd — the cheapest way to speed up 44 lakh daily bus trips.

items.h: One card, one fare, timed transfers
items.p: Integrate BMTC + metro ticketing and fares so a bus-to-metro trip is seamless and not double-charged.
Citywide live bus tracking and published open GTFS feeds, plus a public reliability/punctuality dashboard.
Six-car trains and tighter peak headways; cycle docks and park-and-ride at every station to fix last-mile access.
Give the legislated unified metropolitan transport authority real budget, staff and decision power over all modes.

y3.meta: Capital build-out over ~2–4 years — network expansion, electrification and a true rapid-bus backbone.

items.p: Complete the 75 km Phase 2 and target 20–25 lakh daily metro riders; begin Phase 3 (ORR & airport corridors).
Create Bengaluru's first segregated BRT trunk corridors — the Jakarta/Bogotá move the city has never made.
Scale and electrify the fleet with new depots and charging; aim for a majority-electric bus system.
Single distance-based fare across bus + metro + suburban rail, with monthly passes and concessions.
Push the 54.9 km under construction to operation; unblock land acquisition that has stalled the project.
Transit-oriented zoning and land-value capture around stations to densify ridership and help fund transit.

lt.meta: Structural, 5–10+ year transformation — a mature, integrated, financially self-sustaining network.

items.p: Complete Phase 3/3A so most of the metropolitan area is within walking distance of frequent rail.
A completed commuter-rail spine integrated with metro and bus for regional trips.
A binding, time-bound target that reverses private-vehicle dominance — Bengaluru's version of Jakarta's 60%/2029 goal.
Dense, mixed-use, walkable development along every transit corridor — transit shapes the city, not the other way round.
A dedicated Urban Transport Fund plus value capture and non-fare revenue, so service isn't hostage to annual budgets.
100% electric buses and a service standard that every resident is within 500 m of frequent public transport.

zones.name: Mahadevapura
zones.hot: ORR tech corridor — Marathahalli, Bellandur, KR Puram. Among India's worst gridlock; jobs far outpace transit.
zones.metro: Purple Line reaches Whitefield, but ORR / Bellandur / Sarjapur have no metro yet (Blue Line under construction).
qw: Bus-priority lane on ORR (Silk Board–KR Puram) with high-frequency feeders to Whitefield metro.
Staggered work hours + pooled tech-park shuttles across ORR employers.
lt: TOD, junction redesign and footpaths at Bellandur / Marathahalli.

zones.name: Bommanahalli
zones.hot: Silk Board junction — ~4.5 km/h at peak, among India's worst; gateway to Electronic City and the ORR.
zones.metro: New Yellow Line (2025) serves Silk Board–Electronic City; HSR / Sarjapur side still a gap.
qw: Feeder buses from HSR & Electronic City to new Yellow Line stations.
Silk Board ramp/signal optimisation + dedicated bus bays.
lt: TOD around Electronic City Yellow Line stations.
Sarjapur Road mass transit + complete streets.

zones.name: East
zones.hot: Commercial core — MG Road, Indiranagar, Shivajinagar. Dense, with very high trip attraction.
zones.metro: Well served by the Purple Line (MG Road, Indiranagar, Baiyappanahalli).
qw: Paid parking + pedestrian pilots on MG Rd / Indiranagar 100ft Rd.
Last-mile e-autos and cycles at Purple Line stations.
lt: Off-street parking + timed freight to decongest the core.

zones.name: South
zones.hot: Dense residential-commercial — Jayanagar, BTM, Banashankari, Bannerghatta Road.
zones.metro: Green + Yellow lines give decent coverage; Bannerghatta Road is the main gap.
qw: Bus lanes + frequency boost on Bannerghatta Road.
Seamless Green–Yellow interchange and last-mile links.

zones.name: Yelahanka
zones.hot: Hebbal junction and airport-road peaks; among the fastest-growing peripheries.
zones.metro: No operational metro yet — the Airport (Blue) Line is under construction.
qw: Bus priority and throughput fixes at Hebbal junction.
lt: Operationalise the Airport Metro + suburban-rail corridor.
TOD-led peripheral growth to avoid car dependence.

zones.name: West
zones.hot: Yeshwantpur, Rajajinagar, Malleshwaram; heavy wholesale-market and rail-freight movement.
zones.metro: Green + Purple lines serve the spine well.
qw: Bus priority around Yeshwantpur / goods-shed area.
Last-mile connections at Green & Purple stations.
lt: TOD intensification along the Green Line.

zones.name: Dasarahalli
zones.hot: Peenya industrial belt and Tumkur Road freight congestion.
zones.metro: Green Line (Peenya, Jalahalli, Madavara) gives reasonable coverage.
qw: Peenya industrial-area shuttle + Tumkur Road bus priority.
lt: Suburban rail on the Tumkur Road corridor.

zones.name: RR Nagar
zones.hot: Kengeri, RR Nagar, Mysuru Road; a large zone with thin coverage off the main corridor.
zones.metro: Purple Line serves Kengeri–Challaghatta; coverage thins away from the spine.
qw: Feeder buses to the Purple Line (Kengeri / Challaghatta).
lt: TOD around Kengeri terminal + suburban rail.
